briefdescription.para: Creates an uninitialized CameraActor handle.

detaileddescription.para: Initialize it using CameraActor::New(). Calling member functions with an uninitialized CameraActor handle is not allowed. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual, since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Copy constructor.
Assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
The actor to copy
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Move constructor.
Move assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_2.4
rhs
A reference to the actor to move
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Sets the camera type. The default type is Dali::Camera::FREE_LOOK @SINCE_1_0.0.
Gets the type of the camera.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The type of camera

briefdescription.para: Sets the projection mode.
Gets the projection mode.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
One of PERSPECTIVE_PROJECTION or ORTHOGRAPHIC_PROJECTION

briefdescription.para: Sets the field of view. Field of view will be used when ProjectionMode is PERSPECTIVE_PROJECTION.
Gets the field of view in Radians.

detaileddescription.para:
The default field of view is 45 degrees. @SINCE_1_0.0
The field of view in radians

briefdescription.para: Sets the aspect ratio.
Gets the aspect ratio of the camera.

detaileddescription.para:
The default aspect ratio is 4.0f/3.0f. @SINCE_1_0.0
The aspect ratio

briefdescription.para: Sets the near clipping plane distance.
Gets the near clipping plane distance.

detaileddescription.para:
The default near clipping plane is 800.0f, to match the default screen height. Reduce this value to see objects closer to the camera. @SINCE_1_0.0
The near clipping plane value

briefdescription.para: Sets the far clipping plane distance.
Gets the far clipping plane distance.

detaileddescription.para:
The default value is the default near clipping plane + (0xFFFF>>4). @SINCE_1_0.0
The far clipping plane value

briefdescription.para: Sets the target position of the camera.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
targetPosition
The position of the target to look at
Camera type is LOOK_AT_TARGET.

briefdescription.para: Gets the Camera Target position.

detaileddescription.para:
The default target position is Vector3::ZERO. @SINCE_1_0.0
The target position of the camera
Camera type is LOOK_AT_TARGET.

briefdescription.para: Requests for an inversion on the Y axis on the projection calculation.

detaileddescription.para:
The default value is not inverted. @SINCE_1_0.0
invertYAxis
True if the Y axis should be inverted

briefdescription.para: Gets whether the Y axis is inverted.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
True if the Y axis is inverted, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Sets the default camera perspective projection for the given canvas size.

detaileddescription.para: Sets the near and far clipping planes, the field of view, the aspect ratio, and the Z position of the actor based on the canvas size so that 1 unit in XY (z=0) plane is 1 pixel on screen. Also, It will set orthographic size be fitted as XY plane.

@SINCE_1_0.0
size
The canvas size
The canvas size must be greater than zero.
If either of the values of size is 0.0f, then we use the default perspective projection for the size of the scene this actor is added to.
This modifies the Z position property of this actor as well.

briefdescription.para: Sets the camera projection to use orthographic projection.

detaileddescription.para: The XY plane is centered on the camera axis. The units in the X/Y plane directly equate to pixels on an equivalently sized framebuffer.
The Z position of the actor, and the near and far clip planes of the bounding box match those that would be created by using SetPerspectiveProjection with the same size.

briefdescription.para: Creates a CameraActor object.

detaileddescription.para:
Sets the default camera perspective projection for the size of the scene this is added to.
SetPerspectiveProjection().
When this actor gets added to a scene, then it's Z position will be modified according to the required perspective projection. After modified Z position, 1-world-unit become 1-pixel.
@SINCE_1_0.0
The newly created camera actor

briefdescription.para: Creates a CameraActor object.

detaileddescription.para:
Sets the default camera perspective projection for the given canvas size.
SetPerspectiveProjection().
@SINCE_1_0.0
size
The canvas size
The newly created camera actor

briefdescription.para: Creates a CameraActor object initialize by 3D parameter.

detaileddescription.para: Intialize default perspective camera s.t. properties as good to be used on 3D app case. Default camera positioned at +Z axis, and fit to see 1-world-unit sized cube on world origin.

@SINCE_2_2.15
The newly created camera actor

briefdescription.para: Downcasts a handle to CameraActor handle.

detaileddescription.para:
If handle points to a CameraActor, the downcast produces valid handle. If not, the returned handle is left uninitialized. @SINCE_1_0.0
handle
to An object
Handle to a CameraActor or an uninitialized handle

briefdescription.para: CameraActor controls a camera.

detaileddescription.para: Allows the developer to use actor semantics to control a camera.
DALi has a concept of a camera to display its virtual 3D world to a 2D screen. There are 2 ways of using the camera in DALi:

listitem.para: For 2D applications, you do not need to care about the camera at all. The default camera is already best suited for 2D applications (configured to have the origin of the coordinate system at the top-left corner of the screen, and unit 1 as 1 pixel of the screen). This is a typical way.

For 3D applications, you can change the view by manipulating the camera. If you config some camera properties, or create camera by
New3DCamera(), you can translate or rotate the camera whatever you want. Note that the top-left corner of the screen and unit 1 no longer are (0,0,0) and 1 pixel after manipulating the camera.
There are two types of camera actor, FREE_LOOK and LOOK_AT_TARGET. By default, the camera actor will be FREE_LOOK.
A FREE_LOOK camera uses actor's orientation to control where the camera is looking. If no additional rotations are specified, the camera looks in the negative Z direction.
For LOOK_AT_TARGET, the actor's orientation is ignored, instead the camera looks at TARGET_POSITION in world coordinates.

detaileddescription.para: @SINCE_1_0.0
